title: 10 best herbal teas for inflamed mouth

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with inflamed mouth because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The menthol in peppermint oil reduces swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed mouth conditions such as gingivitis or stomatitis. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint tea can help numb the area, providing temporary relief from discomfort. As a natural antibacterial agent, peppermint tea also helps combat oral infections that may contribute to inflammation, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the mouth, making it effective in soothing inflamed gums.

Antibacterial properties: The menthol in peppermint tea has antibacterial properties that can help combat the growth of bacteria that can cause infections and inflammation in the mouth, reducing the risk of infection.

Cooling and analgesic effects: Peppermint tea's cooling sensation helps reduce discomfort and pain in the mouth by numbing the affected area and providing temporary relief from inflammation, making it a natural remedy for mouth sores and gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, which helps numb and reduce pain in the mouth.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or purchase dried peppermint herb.
Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint herb or two teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves.
Heat one cup of water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches 195°f to 205°f.
Steep the measured peppermint herb in the hot water for five to seven minutes.
Strain and serve the tea while warm, drinking at least three cups daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation in the mouth. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause inflamed mouth. Drinking herbal teas made from Kansas coneflower may provide relief from mouth sores, gum inflammation, and other oral issues associated with inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Antioxidants in Kansas Coneflower tea help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, alleviating discomfort and swelling in the mouth.

Anti-inflammatory flavonoids: Flavonoids present in the tea, such as quercetin and kaempferol, possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce redness, pain, and swelling associated with an inflamed mouth.

Tannins and polyphenols: Tannins and polyphenols in Kansas Coneflower tea have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat bacteria and other pathogens responsible for causing infections and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed mouth tissues.
Rhyzomes: Containing sesquiterpenes, these parts can provide a natural analgesic effect, reducing pain and discomfort associated with an inflamed mouth.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers into a tea infuser or small muslin bag.
Combine the echinacea flowers with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Drink the echinacea angustifolia tea immediately after preparation or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herbs present in comfrey tea, such as allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and caffeic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with inflamed mouth tissues. Additionally, comfrey's antioxidant properties help protect the oral mucosa from further damage caused by inflammation, promoting a faster healing process. As a result, drinking comfrey tea can provide relief from mouth sores, gum inflammation, and other conditions characterized by inflammation and discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey tea contains compounds like allantoin, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Antibacterial properties: The tea's antibacterial agents can combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the inflammation in the mouth, promoting a healthier oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Allantoin and other compounds in comfrey tea can stimulate collagen production, aid in tissue regeneration, and promote wound healing, which may help soothe and repair inflamed mouth tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a compound that soothes and protects mucous membranes, making them ideal for reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the mouth.
Barks: Containing compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to combat oral infections and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried leaves of symphytum officinale plants for a minimum of 2 ounces.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried herb or two teaspoons of fresh herb for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Bring the water to a boil and then reduce heat to a simmer for five minutes allowing flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose herbs and sediment.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming as needed to alleviate inflamed mouth discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with inflamed mouth because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The tea's flavonoids, specifically luteolin and apigenin, work together to reduce swelling and discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant-rich compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation, promoting a faster recovery from mouth sores and inflammation. As a result, this herbal tea provides natural relief for those suffering from inflamed mouths.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds: Calendula's flavonoids, terpenes, and triterpenoids have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation, making it effective in soothing mouth sores and gum inflammation.

Anti-oxidant properties: Marigold tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate oral inflammation and damage, promoting a healthy mouth environment and reducing oxidative stress.

Wound-healing and tissue-repairing agents: Calendula contains compounds like sesquiterpenes, which stimulate collagen synthesis, promote wound healing, and reduce scarring, making it beneficial for treating inflamed mouth sores and gum wounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe sore gums, making them an effective ingredient in teas for mouth sores.
Barks: The barks of the pot marigold plant have been traditionally used to treat gum disease and toothache, providing relief from inflamed and painful mouths.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in quantities of 2-3 teaspoons per cup water.
Combine the harvested calendula flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant container for 10-15 minutes steeping time.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for storage or consuming immediately.
Drink one cup of the cooled calendula tea 2-3 times daily for a duration of 7-10 days to alleviate inflamed mouth symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. The tea's berberine content also reduces swelling and eases pain associated with inflamed gums. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from discomfort and reducing redness and irritation. By drinking goldenseal tea regularly, individuals can promote healthy gum tissue and reduce the risk of further inflammation.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key alkaloid found in goldenseal tea, has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and infection in the mouth caused by bacteria or viruses.

Berbamine: Berbamine, another alkaloid present in goldenseal tea, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with mouth sores, gum disease, and other oral infections.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids found in goldenseal tea, such as berberine and wogonin, have been shown to possess antibacterial properties, which can help combat bacterial overgrowth and reduce inflammation in the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant are rich in berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to soothe and calm an inflamed mouth.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of goldenseal contain berberine and other compounds that work together to reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality goldenseal roots measuring approximately one tablespoon for every cup of water.
Rinse the roots in cold running water and gently scrub off any dirt or debris.
Boil fresh water in a pot and add the cleaned goldenseal roots for a steeping time of 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, taking up to three cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe swollen tissues in the mouth. Additionally, dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation. As a result, dandelion tea can provide relief from gum disease, mouth sores, and other inflammatory conditions, promoting a healthier and more comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and taraxanthin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the mouth, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to oral tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Astringent properties: Dandelion tea's astringent compounds, like tannins, help shrink swollen tissue and reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels, providing quick relief from mouth sores and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing sore gums.
Buds: Contains bioactive compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with inflamed mouth tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired tea dosage of one teaspoon per cup.
Combine one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves with one cup of boiling water and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining loose herb particles.
Allow the strained tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before consumption within 30 minutes.
Drink the prepared taraxacum officinale tea thrice daily for a minimum of three consecutive days for optimal relief from inflamed mouth symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb glycyrrhizin, found in licorice root, has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with oral inflammation. When consumed as a tea, the compounds work to soothe irritated tissues and calm the mucous membranes, providing relief from conditions such as gingivitis, mouth sores, and dry mouth. This natural remedy can help to promote healing, reduce discomfort, and leave the mouth feeling fresh and healthy once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, which help reduce inflammation in the mouth, alleviating conditions like gum inflammation, mouth sores, and sore throats.
Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi that contribute to mouth inflammation, such as thrush or mouth ulcers.

Soothing demulcent properties: Licorice tea's soothing demulcent properties coat and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, providing relief from discomfort, pain, and irritation associated with inflamed mouth conditions like oral lesions or mucositis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe soreness in the mouth.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with inflamed mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots weighing approximately one ounce.
Measure two teaspoons of dried roots and add it to a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 212°f and pour it over the root mixture slowly.
Allow the mixture to steep for ten to fifteen minutes to release active compounds fully.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup and consume immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the mucous membranes of the mouth. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help to soothe sore gums and mouths, reducing pain and discomfort caused by inflamed mouth tissues. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in ginger tea, including vitamin C and E, can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate inflammation and damage to oral tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots from your garden or local market weighing approximately 2 ounces.
Rinse the ginger roots under cool running water to remove any dirt or debris and then pat dry with paper towels.
Peel the ginger roots using a vegetable peeler and slice into thin strips roughly 1/8 inch thick measuring about 4 inches long.
Combine the sliced ginger strips with 2 cups of boiling water in a medium-sized pot and let steep for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reaches a comfortable temperature for drinking.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot discarding the solids and serve hot to help alleviate inflamed mouth symptoms within an hour.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried marshmallow root, 1 quart of boiling water, and a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the marshmallow root in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired, and stir well to combine.
Let the tea cool for 5-10 minutes before drinking it slowly and thoroughly to help soothe inflamed mouth.

Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before using it as a mouthwash twice daily or drinking it slowly as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with inflamed mouth because its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling. The tea's antioxidant content also helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation. Additionally, stinging nettle's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and discomfort. As a result, drinking stinging nettle teas can provide quick relief from mouth sores, gum inflammation, and other oral inflammatory issues, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with an inflamed mouth.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing the inflammation in the mouth, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Stinging nettle tea's rich antioxidant content helps protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm irritated oral tissues.
Barks: Contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried nettle leaves and one cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried nettle leaves in the boiling water for five minutes and ten seconds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste as needed for sweetening and anti-inflammatory properties.
Enjoy the herbal tea immediately and repeat the process every six hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids, particularly rosmarinic acid, have been shown to effectively reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with mouth inflammation. Additionally, lemon balm's antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to inflamed mouths. As the soothing brew is sipped, its calming effects can ease discomfort and promote a healthier oral environment, making it an ideal natural remedy for managing inflamed mouths.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon balm tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, soothing symptoms such as gum swelling and pain.

Soothing and calming effects on the nervous system: The essential oils in lemon balm tea have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related tension that may exacerbate mouth inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm tea's antimicrobial compounds may help combat oral infections such as gingivitis and periodontitis by reducing the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, thereby preventing further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory properties to soothe mouth irritations and reduce pain.
Leaves: Containing high levels of rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that helps to calm inflammation and promote healing in the oral cavity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for immediate use or dry them properly for later use.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis herb or two teaspoons of fresh leafy material per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbal material in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes or allow it to simmer for an additional 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to prevent further mouth irritation.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it three times daily, ideally between meals, for optimal anti-inflammatory benefits.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for inflamed mouth are natural remedies that soothe and calm oral inflammation. These teas help to reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with conditions like oral herpes, canker sores, and gum inflammation. Examples of herbal teas that help with inflamed mouth include peppermint, chamomile, and slippery elm. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, these teas can improve lives by restoring oral health and confidence.
